name: adafruit-circuitpython-datetime
documentation:
# Introduction

```{image} https://readthedocs.org/projects/datetime/badge/?version=latest
:alt: Documentation Status
:target: https://docs.circuitpython.org/projects/datetime/en/latest/
```

```{image} https://raw.githubusercontent.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_Bundle/main/badges/adafruit_discord.svg
:alt: Discord
:target: https://adafru.it/discord
```

```{image} https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_datetime/workflows/Build%20CI/badge.svg
:alt: Build Status
:target: https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_datetime/actions
```

```{image} https://img.shields.io/badge/code%20style-black-000000.svg
:alt: 'Code Style: Black'
:target: https://github.com/psf/black
```

Basic date and time types. Implements a subset of the [CPython datetime module](https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html).

NOTE: This library has a large memory footprint and is intended for hardware such as the SAMD51, ESP32-S2, and nRF52.

# Dependencies

This driver depends on:

- [Adafruit CircuitPython](https://github.com/adafruit/circuitpython)

Please ensure all dependencies are available on the CircuitPython filesystem.
This is easily achieved by downloading
[the Adafruit library and driver bundle](https://circuitpython.org/libraries).

# Installing from PyPI

On supported GNU/Linux systems like the Raspberry Pi, you can install the driver locally [from
PyPI](https://pypi.org/project/adafruit-circuitpython-datetime/). To install for current user:

```shell
pip3 install adafruit-circuitpython-datetime
```

To install system-wide (this may be required in some cases):

```shell
sudo pip3 install adafruit-circuitpython-datetime
```

To install in a virtual environment in your current project:

```shell
mkdir project-name && cd project-name
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip3 install adafruit-circuitpython-datetime
```

# Usage Example

```python
# Example of working with a `datetime` object
# from https://docs.python.org/3/library/datetime.html#examples-of-usage-datetime
from adafruit_datetime import datetime, date, time

# Using datetime.combine()
d = date(2005, 7, 14)
print(d)
t = time(12, 30)
print(datetime.combine(d, t))

# Using datetime.now()
print("Current time (GMT +1):", datetime.now())

# Using datetime.timetuple() to get tuple of all attributes
dt = datetime(2006, 11, 21, 16, 30)
tt = dt.timetuple()
for it in tt:
    print(it)

print("Today is: ", dt.ctime())

iso_date_string = "2020-04-05T05:04:45.752301"
print("Creating new datetime from ISO Date:", iso_date_string)
isodate = datetime.fromisoformat(iso_date_string)
print("Formatted back out as ISO Date: ", isodate.isoformat())
```

# Documentation

API documentation for this library can be found on [Read the Docs](https://docs.circuitpython.org/projects/datetime/en/latest/).

For information on building library documentation, please check out [this guide](https://learn.adafruit.com/creating-and-sharing-a-circuitpython-library/sharing-our-docs-on-readthedocs#sphinx-5-1).

# Contributing

Contributions are welcome! Please read our [Code of Conduct](https://github.com/adafruit/Adafruit_CircuitPython_datetime/blob/main/CODE_OF_CONDUCT.md)
before contributing to help this project stay welcoming.

modules.name: adafruit_datetime
documentation:
`adafruit_datetime`
================================================================================
Concrete date/time and related types.

See http://www.iana.org/time-zones/repository/tz-link.html for
time zone and DST data sources.

Implementation Notes
--------------------

**Software and Dependencies:**

* Adafruit CircuitPython firmware for the supported boards:
  https://github.com/adafruit/circuitpython/releases
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 2001-2021 Python Software Foundation.All rights reserved.
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 1995-2001 Corporation for National Research Initiatives.
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 1995-2001 Corporation for National Research Initiatives.
SPDX-FileCopyrightText: 1991-1995 Stichting Mathematisch Centrum. All rights reserved.

functions.name: adafruit_datetime._check_tzname
documentation: "Just raise TypeError if the arg isn't None or a string.

classes.name: adafruit_datetime.timedelta

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.timedelta.total_seconds
documentation: Return the total number of seconds contained in the duration.
A timedelta object represents a duration, the difference between two dates or times.

classes.name: adafruit_datetime.tzinfo

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.tzinfo.utcoffset
documentation:
Return offset of local time from UTC, as a timedelta
object that is positive east of UTC.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.tzinfo.tzname
documentation: Return the time zone name corresponding to the datetime object dt, as a string.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.tzinfo.dst
documentation:
Return the DST setting correspinding to the datetime object dt, as a number.

DST usage is currently not implemented for this library.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.tzinfo.fromutc
documentation: tzinfo is an abstract base class, disabling for self._offset

This is an abstract base class, meaning that this class should not
be instantiated directly. Define a subclass of tzinfo to capture information
about a particular time zone.

classes.name: adafruit_datetime.date

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.date.__new__
documentation:
Creates a new date object.

:param int year: Year within range, MINYEAR <= year <= MAXYEAR
:param int month: Month within range, 1 <= month <= 12
:param int day: Day within range, 1 <= day <= number of days in the given month and year

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.date.day
documentation: Between 1 and the number of days in the given month of the given year.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.date.fromtimestamp
documentation:
Return the local date corresponding to the POSIX timestamp,
such as is returned by time.time().

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.date.fromordinal
documentation:
Return the date corresponding to the proleptic Gregorian ordinal,
where January 1 of year 1 has ordinal 1.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.date.fromisoformat
documentation:
Return a date object constructed from an ISO date format.
Valid format is ``YYYY-MM-DD``

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.date.replace
documentation:
Return a date with the same value, except for those parameters
given new values by whichever keyword arguments are specified.
If no keyword arguments are specified - values are obtained from
datetime object.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.date.timetuple
documentation:
Return a time.struct_time such as returned by time.localtime().
The hours, minutes and seconds are 0, and the DST flag is -1.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.date.toordinal
documentation:
Return the proleptic Gregorian ordinal of the date, where January 1 of
year 1 has ordinal 1.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.date.weekday
documentation: Return the day of the week as an integer, where Monday is 0 and Sunday is 6.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.date.isoweekday
documentation: Return the day of the week as an integer, where Monday is 1 and Sunday is 7.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.date.isoformat
documentation: Return a string representing the date in ISO 8601 format, YYYY-MM-DD:

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.date.__repr__
documentation: Convert to formal string, for repr().

A date object represents a date (year, month and day) in an idealized calendar,
the current Gregorian calendar indefinitely extended in both directions.
Objects of this type are always naive.

classes.name: adafruit_datetime.timezone

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.timezone._create
documentation: High-level creation for a timezone object.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.timezone.__repr__
documentation: Convert to formal string, for repr().

The timezone class is a subclass of tzinfo, each instance of which represents a
timezone defined by a fixed offset from UTC.

Objects of this class cannot be used to represent timezone information in the locations
where different offsets are used in different days of the year or where historical changes
have been made to civil time.

classes.name: adafruit_datetime.time

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.time.tzinfo
documentation:
The object passed as the tzinfo argument to
the time constructor, or None if none was passed.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.time.fromisoformat
documentation:
Return a time object constructed from an ISO date format.
Valid format is ``HH[:MM[:SS[.fff[fff]]]][+HH:MM[:SS[.ffffff]]]``

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.time.isoformat
documentation:
Return a string representing the time in ISO 8601 format, one of:
HH:MM:SS.ffffff, if microsecond is not 0

HH:MM:SS, if microsecond is 0

HH:MM:SS.ffffff+HH:MM[:SS[.ffffff]], if utcoffset() does not return None

HH:MM:SS+HH:MM[:SS[.ffffff]], if microsecond is 0 and utcoffset() does not return None

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.time.utcoffset
documentation:
Return the timezone offset in minutes east of UTC (negative west of
UTC).

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.time.tzname
documentation:
Return the timezone name.

Note that the name is 100% informational -- there's no requirement that
it mean anything in particular. For example, "GMT", "UTC", "-500",
"-5:00", "EDT", "US/Eastern", "America/New York" are all valid replies.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.time._tzstr
documentation: Return formatted timezone offset (+xx:xx) or None.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.time.__repr__
documentation: Convert to formal string, for repr().

A time object represents a (local) time of day, independent of
any particular day, and subject to adjustment via a tzinfo object.

classes.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.day
documentation: Between 1 and the number of days in the given month of the given year.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.tzinfo
documentation:
The object passed as the tzinfo argument to the datetime constructor,
or None if none was passed.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime._fromtimestamp
documentation:
Construct a datetime from a POSIX timestamp (like time.time()).
A timezone info object may be passed in as well.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.fromisoformat
documentation:
Return a datetime object constructed from an ISO date format.
Valid format is ``YYYY-MM-DD[*HH[:MM[:SS[.fff[fff]]]][+HH:MM[:SS[.ffffff]]]]``

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.now
documentation: Return the current local date and time.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.utcfromtimestamp
documentation: Return the UTC datetime corresponding to the POSIX timestamp, with tzinfo None

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.combine
documentation:
Return a new datetime object whose date components are equal to the
given date object’s, and whose time components are equal to the given time object’s.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.date
documentation: Return date object with same year, month and day.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.time
documentation:
Return time object with same hour, minute, second, microsecond and fold.
tzinfo is None. See also method timetz().

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.dst
documentation:
If tzinfo is None, returns None, else returns self.tzinfo.dst(self),
and raises an exception if the latter doesn’t return None or a timedelta
object with magnitude less than one day.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.timetuple
documentation: Return local time tuple compatible with time.localtime().

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.utcoffset
documentation:
If tzinfo is None, returns None, else returns
self.tzinfo.utcoffset(self), and raises an exception
if the latter doesn’t return None or a timedelta object
with magnitude less than one day.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.toordinal
documentation: Return the proleptic Gregorian ordinal of the date.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.weekday
documentation: Return the day of the week as an integer, where Monday is 0 and Sunday is 6.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.__repr__
documentation: Convert to formal string, for repr().

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.isoformat
documentation:
Return a string representing the date and time in
ISO8601 format.

methods.name: adafruit_datetime.datetime.replace
documentation:
Return a datetime with the same attributes,
except for those attributes given new values by
whichever keyword arguments are specified.

A datetime object is a single object containing all the information
from a date object and a time object. Like a date object, datetime assumes
the current Gregorian calendar extended in both directions; like a time object,
datetime assumes there are exactly 3600*24 seconds in every day.
